text: That's role. And I'm looking at you, Rich.
When I worked at Robin Hood, I bumped into my dentist
and he knew that I was looking for something.
So he asked me, do you want to see how I run my finances?
And I said, absolutely.
He walked me to his back office and it looked like the 70s all over again.
I left Robin Hood and then took a 9-5 job in Dr. Kwan's office.
The more time we spent with Dr. Kwan, the more we were convinced
that if we can help them put their business on autopilot
that would be like a massive opportunity to pursue, so we couldn't resist.
I remember like I kind of couldn't fathom that people were doing this every day.
It just felt like an insurmountable mountain, but I was like stuck on a treadmill.
I just could never catch up.
In the beginning it was me solo doing everything.
Appointments, collecting money, a lot of hours dedicated to non-clinical aspects of practice.
The most doctors that we met, they really just wanted to kind of serve their community
and be a great doctor.
As a result of opening their own office, they basically had to spend all their day
putting up job postings, doing all this paperwork.
I think we got really excited about what does it look like
if these practices just kind of run on their own and the doctors can go back to being doctors.
We thought by hanging out with these doctors and working in their practices,
we had a good idea on how to do it.
It took us almost a year not writing any code.
We talked to primary care physicians.
We worked for a chastronologist in Scranton, Pennsylvania.
We punched in a million dollars in insurance payments, type, type, type, type, type,
until we really understood, oh, this is how you do it.
And then we started building software.
We went on this roadshow together, Frederic and I, because we thought,
okay, we've built a really great first product, we need to install it
and like work with all these different doctors across the country.
We realized that we needed to build something that would just run on its own.
This thing needs to work reliably, whether or not somebody is checking on it.
Every month we're saving over 100 hours.
And that's just really freed us up to focus on the patient experience.
And seeing what Lassie can do is incredible
and is something that I can't imagine any practice won't benefit tremendously.
Our goal is the small business that runs itself.
If we succeed helping all the dental practices and then all the health practices,
we will go on and help all the small businesses in the world run their business.
